<insights>

- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected
- Perfect cohesion and zero coupling are expected and appropriate for static binary and HTML assets
- The presence of dual-mode logos (lightmode/darkmode) indicates theme-switching support is built into the viewer shell
- These assets should be verified as correctly referenced by the viewer build output to avoid broken image references in production
- Dual-mode logo assets (lightmode_logo.png, darkmode_logo.png) correctly support theme switching at the asset level — no code changes needed.
- Ensure the build pipeline copies or hashes these static assets correctly so logo references in index.html resolve after bundling.
- This zone is purely presentational with no logic; it will remain stable unless branding changes, making it a low-maintenance artefact.
- Serves as the static shell for the viewer sub-application while web-landing-page serves the marketing/entry surface — the two static zones represent parallel, non-overlapping entry points into the web package
- viewer-shell-assets and web-landing-page are parallel static entry points with no coupling between them or to any logic zone. This two-entry-point structure is clean; verify that their build outputs are kept independent to avoid accidental asset bundling across the two surfaces.
- index.html statically references asset paths that are resolved only after the build pipeline runs — any mismatch between HTML asset references and build output paths creates a broken runtime dependency that is invisible to the import graph and uncatchable by zone-level analysis
- index.html contains hard-coded asset paths (logo filenames) that must stay in sync with build output — there is no compile-time verification that these references resolve, creating a latent deployment breakage risk invisible to the module graph
- Zone "logo" was identified from filename patterns, not directory structure — consider creating packages/web/src/viewer/logo/ and moving related files there

</insights>
